text.title: Symbol Index Stays Fresh or CI Fails
text.summary: The symbol index is a build artifact that lives in the repo and must equal a freshly regenerated copy at every commit and every CI run.

section.title: Drift-checked symbol index
section.p: If the index drifts from the live AST, agents silently load wrong spans, miss new symbols, and call into stale signatures. Treating the index like generated code (regenerate, diff, fail on drift) is the same pattern proto-buf, sqlc, and tygo already use successfully.

rule.statement: The repo MUST commit a symbol index (e.g. `tags`, `.serena/symbols.db`, or LSIF/SCIP file) at a known path declared in the project `AGENTS.md`. A `pre-commit` hook MUST regenerate the index from staged changes and re-stage it. CI MUST run `scripts/check_tags_fresh.sh` (or equivalent) — regenerate into a temp dir, `diff` against the committed copy, exit non-zero on any difference. Vendored, generated, and build-output dirs MUST be in the index exclude list.
rule.rationale: Agents that consult an out-of-date index produce confidently wrong answers (point to deleted files, miss new functions). The drift gate is the only mechanic that keeps the artifact trustworthy across long-lived repos and parallel branches. The exclude list keeps the index from being dominated by `node_modules`-class noise.

example.code:
# .pre-commit-config.yaml - repo: local hooks: - id: regen-tags name: regenerate ctags entry: bash -c 'ctags --options=.ctags.d/default.ctags -R . && git add tags' language: system pass_filenames: false stages: [pre-commit]
# .github/workflows/ci.yml - name: tags-fresh run: bash scripts/check_tags_fresh.sh
example.note: Index regenerates pre-commit and is gated in CI; drift is impossible to merge.

example.code: # Manual: developer regenerates `tags` "when they remember" # CI: no check
example.note: The index goes stale within days; agents read pre-refactor symbols and emit broken patches.

antipattern.description: Adding the index to `.gitignore` and rebuilding it in each CI run.
antipattern.reason: Defeats offline / sandboxed agent runtimes (no network or LSP server) and removes the diff signal that catches index-affecting refactors during code review.

section.reference: Universal-ctags config tuned for polyglot repos.
Universal-ctags man page — `--options` and exclude flags.
Serena — LSP-backed symbol index that ships an MCP server agents query directly.
SCIP — SourceGraph's modern indexable symbol format.
